paired_solve_layout_comparison.interpretation: The column-major multiplier solve layout, frequency-ordered fixed dispatch, and fixed-size stack RHS are the only code deltas. Backend time improves in all seven pairs; the smaller end-to-end median and the assembly movement are treated as noise-scale.

paired_single_pass_validation_comparison.interpretation: Single-pass finite validation/copy and one-triangle diagonal symmetry validation are the code deltas. The bordered wrapper delegates stage-block validation to the owning stage solver. End-to-end and backend times improve in all seven pairs; assembly is unchanged within noise.

paired_edge_once_inertia_comparison.interpretation: The independent inertia proof now visits every symmetric off-diagonal edge once and accumulates its magnitude into both Gershgorin rows while preserving exact symmetry checks and the original margins. Backend and assembly improve in all seven pairs and end-to-end time improves in six; reconstruction regresses by only 0.185 ms at the median, a code-layout-scale penalty outweighed by the backend gain.

paired_symmetric_matrix_application_comp.interpretation: The projected symmetric diagonal is now applied one off-diagonal edge at a time, the 12x12 and 14x14 kernels have fixed dispatch, and internal refinement avoids the public alias-safe transactional copies. Backend and assembly medians improve slightly, but the end-to-end median regresses by 0.29% and wins only three pairs; this CPU-timer series is therefore treated as inconclusive rather than as a wall-clock speedup. The isolated microbenchmark and deterministic instruction profile are the kernel gates.

paired_normalized_assembler_comparisons.excluded_series: Two earlier combined reruns are excluded. Sustained profiler heat and system load moved the three-binary series across a 1.70--2.37 second C++23 range; a warmed CPU-10 candidate/previous rerun still had 4.3--7.6% coefficient of variation and only three paired wins. The later straight-line four-state-products native series is also excluded: total CV is 2.25--2.36%, assembly CV is 2.92--3.24%, and assembly wins only three of seven pairs. No result from these series is used as wall-clock performance evidence.
paired_normalized_assembler_comparisons.interpretation: Four accepted wall-clock code deltas were isolated sequentially. Triangular Gram formation is instruction-positive but end-to-end noise-scale; sparse -I writes, fixed four-state products, and the checked fixed four-state inverse improve their own paired medians. The inverse step improves assembly and backend in all seven pairs and total time in five. The subsequent straight-line four-state product helpers are accepted only by deterministic instruction counts because their native series is thermally noisy. Arbitrary state counts retain the generic path and all pivot, singularity, finite-output, and backward-error gates remain live.

fused_inertia_copy_profile.implementation: Accumulate every primal Hessian, local-global border, and cross-stage Gershgorin edge while its matrix coefficient is already being copied. Preserve the original global, physical-stage, and transition accumulation order; copy both Hessian orientations independently and retain exact asymmetry rejection. The remaining certificate pass checks only primal/slack/global diagonals and emits the exact inertia token.

unique_sparse_derivative_scatter_profile.implementation: Build an immutable plan for every sparse-to-packed derivative scatter. A unique-target plan overwrites mapped coefficients and resets only structural holes; Number{0.} + source preserves the former signed-zero result. If any coordinate aliases a target, that plan retains the original full zero-fill and += sequence in source-entry order. Source finite checks remain unconditional, and the packed-output finite scan is skipped only when every externally visible target plan is unique and accumulation overflow is therefore impossible.

fused_jacobian_products_profile.implementation: Add one transactional AnyAny operation for J*v and J^T*w. A model may provide a native fused callback. When neither direction is native, the fallback validates and materializes the Jacobian once, then updates both outputs in the original sparse-entry order. Mixed/native models retain transpose-then-forward dispatch. Every input may alias either output, the two outputs may not overlap, and neither public output is committed unless the complete pair succeeds. The legacy coordinate adapter expands x and both directions once and maps fixed-variable equality contributions after the source pair succeeds.

coordinate_kkt_operator_reuse_profile.implementation: Keep a PrimalDualKktOperator beside the already persistent coordinate AnyNlpProblem for the lifetime of one PDSystemSolver InitializeImpl. Construct the layout, bound maps, NlpKktOperator scratch, and full primal-dual scratch once, then reuse them for later Newton solves. Reset the KKT before replacing or destroying its borrowed coordinate-problem owner. Restoration and every other matrix-snapshot path retain a locally owned transient problem and KKT operator, so no snapshot numeric data crosses solves.

evaluation_vector_workspace_profile.implementation: Replace each owning CopyVector result with direct writes into per-PDSystemSolver contiguous state, flat-RHS, flat-reference, and reconstructed-RHS buffers. Equal Ipopt::Number/C++23 Number builds call FillValuesFromVector directly into the destination and scan it once; mixed-precision builds retain one monotonically grown stable-number conversion scratch. y_c and y_d remain adjacent and are borrowed as one multiplier span. Every span is rebuilt after resize on each request, no request view may outlive the synchronous AnyAny call, and private partially written buffers are ignored after failure. The accepted candidate direction is scaled in place after the unscaled true-residual check instead of being copied.

direct_equal_precision_commit_profile.implementation: CommitDirection receives only the scaled direction already checked finite by EvaluateSolve. When Ipopt::Number and C++23 Number are identical, pass that immutable storage directly to PutValuesInVector instead of allocating, zero-initializing, identity-converting, and rescanning a second full-size vector. When the scalar types differ, grow and reuse the solver-owned stable-number scratch, convert and validate every value before writing any detached block. In both cases all eight blocks are written into a fresh detached IteratesVector and the legacy result remains untouched until the final result.Copy commit.

reconstruction_complementarity_validatio.implementation: Each bordered assembly attempt clears the prior proof, validates all complementarity dual and slack spans, and publishes its nonzero numeric revision only after the complete assembly succeeds. Reconstruction skips its duplicate full-span scan only when the request has exactly that nonzero revision. Revision zero and every changed revision retain the original scan, and the caller contract now explicitly requires advancing the generation whenever any numeric value reachable through the state view changes.

fixed_four_state_reconstruction_profile.implementation: For normalized transitions with exactly four next states, load the four generic-to-structured positions and four normalized direction values once, then emit all four original dynamics directions through a fixed C++23 helper. Each output retains the prior sequential accumulation order from zero through terms 0, 1, 2, and 3. Every other state count retains the original generic nested loop.

certified_full_direction_overwrite_profi.implementation: Add a conservative full-direction overwrite certificate to both structured layouts. Equality, primal-dual stage, and primal-dual bordered-stage assemblers publish it only after live binding/configuration proves complete reduced coverage and complementarity reconstruction. A certified NDEBUG backend skips the redundant full-vector prefill. Uncertified assemblers retain NaN poisoning, every non-NDEBUG build poisons even certified output, and the existing finite-output scan remains unconditional.

caller_owned_candidate_direction_profile.implementation: Allow a candidate-first request to provide optional caller-owned mutable direction storage. Structured backends reconstruct synchronously into that exact-size span, return an explicit output-written marker with an empty owning vector, and never retain the span. The original owning result remains the compatibility fallback. The legacy bridge lends its existing flat-reference workspace only on the candidate path without a reference result, borrows it until the immediate validated commit, and preserves every outer dimension, finite, true-KKT-residual, scaling, and detached-publication gate.

fused_residual_scaling_profile.implementation: Fuse the final true-residual infinity reduction, accepted-direction scaling, and post-scale finite accumulation into one index-ordered EvaluateSolve traversal. The residual difference and scale maxima retain their prior per-index operation order, every direction entry is scaled before the result is inspected, the independent pre-apply unscaled finite gate remains, and reference-direction comparison still runs on the scaled direction.

symmetric_matrix_application_microbenchm.interpretation: All twenty case medians improve and 139 of 140 matched samples are faster. Exact checksums match in every pair, including pivot-heavy matrices.

optimization_delta.interpretation: The candidate-vs-MUMPS block remains the latest thermally stable authoritative protocol comparison. The matrix-application wall-clock comparison is mixed and makes no speedup claim; its direct microbenchmark is the kernel gate. Four later normalized-assembler changes were isolated sequentially: the checked fixed-four-state inverse improves assembly and backend in all seven pairs and total time in five. Subsequent straight-line reconstruction, lifetime/workspace, direct-commit, validation-reuse, and certified-overwrite steps have deterministic instruction evidence only; their single native runs are correctness checks. Exact symmetry, generic-state fallback, public alias safety, detached transactional commit, private-copy failure behavior, inertia, residual, singularity, and pivot gates remain unchanged.

rejected_pivot_row_branch_split_change.description: Replace the row==pivot branch in Gauss--Jordan elimination with two ranges around the pivot row.

pivot_heavy_numeric_microbenchmark_guard.interpretation: Both attempted loop-shape changes lost to the existing contiguous implementation and were fully reverted. The pivot-heavy benchmark remains as a permanent performance and correctness guard.

single_pass_validation_microbenchmark.interpretation: Finite validation is fused with the owning copy, each symmetric diagonal pair is validated and projected once, and the bordered wrapper no longer rescans stage blocks. Numeric size-12 and size-14 gains are consistent; certified factorization remains dominated by the Jacobi path.

comparison.interpretation: The candidate is end-to-end correct, fallback-free, and faster than the coordinate-matched MUMPS baseline in this CPU-pinned series. Its larger accepted perturbation still changes the nonlinear trajectory, so this is an end-to-end result rather than an isolated per-iteration linear-kernel comparison.
